headline: Anthropic Vows Legal Fight Against Pentagon Sanction in AI Feud
summary: “We do not believe this action is legally sound, and we see no choice but to challenge it in court,” Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei said in a blog post Thursday, hours after Bloomberg News reported on the Pentagon finding.  Defense officials had notified the company on Wednesday of the supply-chain designation, Amodei said in his post.  The move puts at risk the firm’s $200 million contract to provide the Pentagon with classified AI tools, and could bar Anthropic from partnering with other companies on their defense work.

content.title: Anthropic Vows Legal Fight Against Pentagon Sanction in AI Feud
content.summary: “We do not believe this action is legally sound, and we see no choice but to challenge it in court,” Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei said in a blog post Thursday, hours after Bloomberg News reported on the Pentagon finding. The move puts at risk the firm’s $200 million contract to provide the Pentagon with classified AI tools, and could bar Anthropic from partnering with other companies on their defense work.

headline: Palantir Confronts AI Model Upheaval As New Government Deals Raise Questions
summary: Palantir Technologies (NasdaqGS:PLTR) is being required by a new U.S. government directive to unwind Anthropic's Claude AI models from core military software platforms. The company is managing this operational shift while securing a Department of Homeland Security contract and a major Australian defence agreement. Senior insiders have recently sold shares as geopolitical conflict and market volatility continue to affect sentiment toward defense focused technology names. Palantir builds data...

headline: Foreign outflows from Indian IT stocks at 7-month high in February on AI shockwaves
summary: "The IT sector is facing multiple headwinds, particularly from the rapid advancement ⁠of AI tools," said Piyush Gupta, fund manager at AlphaGrep Investment Management.  Constructive collaborations between Indian IT firms and global AI leaders, such as the strategic partnership between Infosys and Anthropic, and ​improvement in ​earnings in the sector will be crucial to ​restore FPI interest in the sector, ‌according to three analysts.  The rebound in broader foreign appetite was fueled by improving corporate earnings and easing trade tensions after India sealed a key trade deal with ‌the European Union and an interim framework for an ​agreement with the U.S.

headline: Defense-tech stocks are the hot trade as Iran conflict widens
summary: In a week when conflict in Iran sent the U.S. equity market into a tailspin, technology stocks tied to cybersecurity and artificial intelligence have proved more resilient than sectors investors have typically treated as safe havens.
